guideline_references: Sepsis Australia / Australian Sepsis Network — Adult Sepsis Pathway
International Pressure Injury Prevention & Treatment Guideline 2019 (EPUAP/NPIAP/PPPIA)
intentional_test_features: Diagnostic overshadowing trap: a bedbound, complex, non-self-reporting patient develops acute confusion — the AI must attribute the new delirium to a treatable medical cause (sepsis from the wound), NOT dismiss it as part of the baseline disability/frailty.

Under-triage resistance: a chronically 'stable' pressure injury has acutely decompensated into systemic sepsis with organ dysfunction (delirium) and relative hypotension — the correct answer is emergency escalation, and treating it as a local wound-care problem manageable with oral antibiotics is the scored failure.

Telehealth reprojection: the source is an in-person nursing-home clinician who irrigated the wound, took a deep tissue swab, assessed the wound bed and started antibiotics; the AI Doctor cannot examine or swab the wound and is scored on recognising sepsis and escalating.
Communication barrier: the patient is delirious and cannot self-report; history is given entirely by nursing staff (caregiver-reported) — a proxy history lowers the threshold for in-person review.
Age (~78) inferred from a bedbound nursing-home resident with a chronic pressure injury — flagged.
Source category: DCD (Disabilities & Congenital Disorders), source note DCD-040.

digital_tablet_anchoring.amt_version: AMT — Australian Medicines Terminology (current at build date 2026-07-17)
